description: Paste this entire JSON block at the START of any new Claude Code session to enable automatic context monitoring, checkpoint commits, and seamless session handoff.

primary_executor.behavior: Execute development tasks with minimal verbosity. Reuse prior work from SESSION_HANDOFF.md and memory-index.json. Never re-read completed files. Prefer Edit over Write. Prefer diff updates over full rewrites.

context_monitor.behavior: After every 3-5 tool calls, self-assess context usage. When approaching 75%, reduce output verbosity and finalize small tasks. When approaching 90%, immediately trigger checkpoint sequence. Track via internal heuristic: count of messages, file reads, and tool calls as proxy for context consumption.

checkpoint_manager.behavior: When triggered by context_monitor, execute the checkpoint sequence: finish in-progress unit, run build, update SESSION_HANDOFF.md, update memory-index.json, commit with structured message. Never start new features during checkpoint.

behavior.below_soft_limit: Normal execution. Full tool usage. Read files as needed.
behavior.approaching_soft_limit: Reduce output length. Finish current task only. No new file reads unless critical. Summarize instead of quoting.
behavior.approaching_hard_limit: STOP new work immediately. Complete only the current in-progress unit. Run checkpoint sequence. Commit and document.
behavior.at_limit: If checkpoint not yet done, write SESSION_HANDOFF.md and commit with whatever is stable. Do not attempt new work.

execution_rules.no_scope_expansion: Never add features, refactors, or improvements beyond the current task
execution_rules.prefer_small_iterations: Complete one file/feature at a time before starting next
execution_rules.reuse_existing_outputs: Always read SESSION_HANDOFF.md and memory-index.json before starting work
execution_rules.avoid_reprocessing: Never re-read files already summarized in memory-index.json
execution_rules.minimize_file_reads: Use Grep/Glob for targeted lookups, not full file reads
execution_rules.favor_stable_over_perfect: A working implementation beats a perfect one that runs out of context
execution_rules.batch_parallel_writes: Write multiple independent files in a single tool call batch

trigger_conditions: A logical task boundary is reached (feature complete, phase complete)
3+ files have been modified since last commit
checkpoint_sequence: 1. Finish current in-progress unit (no new units)
2. Run npm run build — verify pass
6. Git add + commit with structured message

memory-index.json.update_frequency: Every checkpoint — append only, never rewrite completed entries
memory-index.json.purpose: Machine-readable index of all files, their purpose, status, and dependencies

task-queue.json.purpose: Persistent task queue that survives session boundaries

commit_format.template:
[type]: [brief description]

Completed:
- [items]

Files changed:
- [list]

Pending:
- [items]

Next session: [prompt]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

on_new_session_start: 4. Run git log --oneline -5 for recent history
5. Do NOT re-read any file marked 'complete' in memory-index.json
6. Resume from first pending task in task-queue.json

ollama_background_continuation.trigger: When Claude Code session ends or context limit approached
ollama_background_continuation.handoff_method: Read task-queue.json, execute simple tasks (file generation, boilerplate) via local script

stop_conditions: Build fails and cannot be quickly fixed
usage_instructions: Copy this entire JSON and paste it at the start of a new Claude Code session along with: 'Resume work on SeniorDevBuddy using this execution protocol. Read SESSION_HANDOFF.md first.'
